soliton.text: Regex character class `[a-zA-Z-_]` in locale-suffix stripping (`_[a-zA-Z-_]*\.properties`) unintentionally creates a `Z-_` range, so matching may silently misbehave for certain characters or future locale formats (e.g., `zh-CN`) and point to the wrong English source file
Locale-suffix stripping regex uses `*` instead of `+`, allowing a pathological filename like `_.properties` to match with an empty suffix

Using `file.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("resources-community", "resources")` is a fragile substring replacement that can rewrite unintended parts of the path (not just the directory segment), causing incorrect `englishFile` resolution and build failures
`verifySafeHtml()` catches `IOException` and rethrows `RuntimeException`, which is inconsistent with the caller’s `MojoExecutionException` handling and results in unhelpful Maven error reporting

Fields `POLICY_SOME_HTML`, `POLICY_NO_HTML`, and `HTML_TAGS` are named like constants but are non-static, non-final, and package-private, causing unnecessary per-instance rebuilding and inconsistent style vs existing `private static final` constants
Method name `santizeAnchors` is misspelled and should be `sanitizeAnchors` (typo at both declaration and call site)

AccessTokenContext constructor mistakenly calls Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed") instead of validating rawTokenId, allowing null rawTokenId and producing token IDs like "onrtac:null" that collide/break downstream operations

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher uses wrong substring indices (substring(3,5) instead of (4,6)) so it reads across tokenType/grantType boundary and does not correctly extract the grant shortcut

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher has inverted boolean logic (returns false when the shortcut matches), causing grant-type assertions to be silently voided and tests to pass for wrong shortcuts/fail for correct ones

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider dereferences userSession without a null-check (clientSessionContext.getClientSession().getUserSession() may be null), leading to NPE/HTTP 500 in token issuance hot path

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory duplicate grant-shortcut detection is broken because ConcurrentHashMap.put overwrites existing entries before size-based collision checks, allowing shortcut collisions to go undetected and causing decode failures later

OAuth2GrantTypeFactory SPI adds getShortcut() as a new abstract method without a default implementation, creating a binary-incompatible change that can trigger AbstractMethodError at startup for third-party/out-of-tree grant plugins

OAuth2GrantType.Context copy constructor (public Context(Context context)) was removed from public SPI without replacement, causing NoSuchMethodError/compile failures for external extensions that clone/wrap Context

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider token-id decoding handles legacy tokens gracefully but throws IllegalArgumentException for unknown encoded-prefix shortcuts, breaking rolling upgrades/heterogeneous clusters by turning unknown shortcuts into HTTP 500 instead of UNKNOWN context

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider.getTokenContextFromTokenId throws on attacker-controlled token IDs and includes the full raw token ID in exception messages, risking HTTP 500s and sensitive/log injection (CWE-209)

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory lazy shortcut-map refresh updates two ConcurrentHashMaps without synchronization, allowing races that break the bijection invariant between grantsByShortcuts and grantsToShortcuts and can miss duplicate shortcuts registered post-startup

AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper changed getShouldUseLightweightToken from an instance method to public static, so subclass overrides are silently ignored (static hiding), causing behavioral regression without compile-time diagnostics
CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential serializes and sends raw plaintext recovery codes to user-storage providers via updateCredential, exposing long-lived bearer secrets in cleartext
RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean calls Optional.get() without checking presence when no recovery credential exists, causing NoSuchElementException and a 500 during login form rendering

CredentialHelper stores a JSON array of raw List<String> as credentialData, but RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel expects hashed RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialData JSON, leading to deserialization/type mismatch and authentication failures/NPEs
BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.isValid validates a recovery code but never removes/invalidates the used code, allowing infinite replay of one-time recovery codes
CredentialHelper hard-codes the provider ID string "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes" instead of using RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialProviderFactory.PROVIDER_ID, risking drift and null provider/NPE

CredentialHelper fallback logic misinterprets updateCredential’s boolean return (handled vs not handled), which can cause double-writes, stale federated data taking precedence, or skipping creation of a properly formed local credential

RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential prefers federated credentials over local without warning, so after adding/migrating to a federated provider a stale/absent federated credential can silently shadow a valid local credential and lock users out

Breaking change to CLI exit-code contract: RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE changed from 4 to 3 and exit code 4 is reused for FEATURE_DISABLED, which can silently break existing automation relying on exit code 4 for 'recreate required'
No deprecation/upgrade documentation for the exit-code contract change (missing upgrading-guide entry)
Test testFeatureNotEnabled only asserts the error message and does not assert the CLI exit code, leaving the numeric exit-code contract untested and prone to regression

Custom-image Dockerfile hardcodes --features=rolling-updates into kc.sh build, which silently enables a preview feature for all derived images and may contradict the intended feature gating if the Dockerfile is user-facing or undocumented

ClientRemovedEvent cleanup path calls ClientPermissionsV2.setPermissionsEnabled(client, false), which unconditionally throws UnsupportedOperationException in V2 and can abort client deletion transactions

AdminPermissions.registerListener wraps role/client/group removal cleanup in ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ feature flag only, causing deployments using other fine-grained-authz flag variants to skip cleanup and leak orphan Resource/Policy records

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel, String) fallback uses AdminPermissionsSchema.getResourceTypeResource(...) without a null check and can throw NullPointerException when the wildcard Clients resource is missing

ClientPermissionsV2.getClientsWithPermission omits clients granted access via the wildcard Clients resource because it only iterates per-client resources returned by findByType, leading to under-reporting authorized clients

ClientPermissionsV2 canMapRoles/canMapCompositeRoles/canMapClientScopeRoles lack the MANAGE_CLIENTS admin-role fast-path present in V1, causing backwards-incompatible denials unless explicit MAP_ROLES policies exist

ClientPermissionsV2 canView(ClientScopeModel)/canManage(ClientScopeModel) reuse the Clients resource type instead of a dedicated ClientScopes resource type, unintentionally broadening client-scope visibility/manage permissions to anyone with client-wide grants
GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats having only the VIEW scope on the all-groups resource as sufficient for MANAGE, enabling read-to-write privilege escalation

getGroupIdsWithViewPermission adds authorization Resource UUIDs (groupResource.getId()) instead of group model IDs (resource name), causing per-group permission checks to miss and fall back to all-groups permissions

getGroupIdsWithViewPermission returns UUIDs that are later used as group model IDs (e.g., UsersResource.getUsersCount and session UserModel.GROUPS attribute), breaking group membership filtering and producing incorrect user results

UsersResource.searchForUser removed the per-user usersEvaluator.canView post-filter and relies only on an optional GROUPS store hint, allowing QUERY_USERS-only callers with no view permissions to enumerate all users when groupIds is empty
BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser has the same missing canView post-filter pattern as UsersResource, creating the same user-enumeration authorization bypass risk

UserPermissionsV2 canView/canManage no longer short-circuit for AdminRoles.ADMIN, potentially denying access (403) to admins in realms where ADMIN is not composed to include manage-users/view-users or where fine-grained policies aren’t configured

AdminPermissions.registerListener now registers the listener only if the feature flag is enabled at registration time, so enabling the feature later (tests/profile reload/reconfig) would skip cleanup events and leave orphaned permission resources in the database

Removal of public static ModelToRepresentation.searchGroupModelsByAttributes is a binary-incompatible API change for downstream extensions; should keep a deprecated delegate or document in migration notes

Adding abstract method order() to public CryptoProvider SPI is source/binary incompatible and can break third-party implementations (compile failures or AbstractMethodError at runtime); should be a default method for backward compatibility

AuthzClientCryptoProvider.getEcdsaCryptoProvider().concatenatedRSToASN1DER contains dead allocations: two ASN1Encoder instances write BigIntegers but their results are discarded, misleading and unnecessary

CryptoIntegration.detectProvider behavior changed from fail-fast on multiple CryptoProviders to silently selecting by highest order(), which can hide misconfiguration and lead to non-deterministic provider selection when orders tie; should throw on ties and/or log at warn

ASN1Decoder.readLength() returns -1 for indefinite-length (0x80) and callers pass it to new byte[length], causing NegativeArraySizeException instead of a clear IOException; DER forbids indefinite length so it should fail fast with IOException

ECDSAAlgorithmTest only tests happy-path roundtrips and lacks malformed-input/negative tests for the new hand-rolled ASN.1 parser (e.g., invalid tags, high-tag-form, indefinite/oversized lengths, wrong element count, length mismatches, truncation), risking untested failure modes in security-sensitive code
GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null, which can cause NullPointerException in callers that auto-unbox to primitive long or call longValue()
getSubGroupsCount() return value violates/changes the GroupModel.getSubGroupsCount() contract by introducing nullable semantics not documented, forcing callers to add unexpected null guards
GroupTest.java reader thread lacks a timeout/join, risking hanging/flaky tests

Possible compile error: UsernameForm.authenticate() calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with zero arguments, but only a one-argument overload (isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)) is defined/visible in the PR; should likely pass context.getUser() or ensure a zero-arg overload exists in the superclass chain
Unused helper method `_create_process_for_shard(self, shard: int)` is defined but never called, risking drift and dead code
Metric tag key is inconsistent for the same shard dimension (`shard` vs `shards`) across emitted metrics, fragmenting dashboards/alerts
Metric tag value shape is inconsistent for the shard dimension (single-shard integer vs comma-joined multi-shard string), causing inconsistent aggregation
`SpanFlusher.__init__` accepts a caller-supplied `SpansBuffer` but effectively discards it (only reads `assigned_shards`), silently dropping any caller initialization/state
Refactor creates multiple `SpansBuffer` instances per shard group (original caller buffer, parent-side buffer, and pickled child-process copy), multiplying Redis client instances/state unnecessarily

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result can slice a Django QuerySet with a negative start offset on backward pagination, triggering Django's guaranteed AssertionError for negative indexing and causing unhandled 500/DoS via attacker-controlled cursor.offset
Inline comments in paginator.py incorrectly claim Django ORM handles negative slicing/boundary conditions automatically, contradicting Django docs and masking the crash risk
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key applies math.floor/math.ceil to a datetime field (from order_by='-datetime'), raising TypeError and crashing audit log pagination during cursor construction
organization_auditlogs.py dereferences organization_context.member.has_global_access without checking for None, causing AttributeError for non-superusers without active org membership

Authorization gate for the advanced/optimized audit log path uses member.has_global_access as a privilege check even though it is not a trust/role indicator, allowing ordinary members (e.g., open-membership orgs) to access the advanced path

BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative forward cursor offsets to 0, silently changing behavior from a loud failure to returning first-page results and potentially hiding cursor-corruption bugs (should be documented/logged)

Comments in cursors.py/paginator.py falsely claim negative offsets enable efficient reverse/bidirectional pagination; negative offsets crash and offset-based reverse pagination is not efficient for large datasets

OptimizedCursorPaginator docstring falsely advertises streamlined boundary handling and an optimized query path despite being a near-copy with crashing branches and no real optimization, misleading maintainers

Lock narrowing in bleveBackend.BuildIndex introduces a TOCTOU race where concurrent calls for the same key can both build an index and the second overwrites/discards the first, potentially corrupting file-backed indexes and double-counting metrics

In file-backed index builds (bleve.New with size > opts.FileThreshold), concurrent same-key builds target the same directory, so the second build may fail or open duplicate handles and leave orphaned/corrupted on-disk state
NewResourceServer now runs synchronous initialization/index building under the caller-provided context, so any deadline/cancellation on that context can abort startup and cause constructor failure

Removing per-method lazy-init guards means callers that ignore NewResourceServer’s error return or construct the server directly (e.g., in tests) lose the previous safety net and may use an uninitialized server

MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but implements none of its required abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data), causing a TypeError on instantiation when metric-alert detectors access .detector_handler

process_detectors assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and calls .values(); any DetectorHandler subclass still returning a list will crash at runtime with AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'values'
Dataclass field `queued` uses `timezone.now()` as a bare default, so it is evaluated once at import time and shared across instances instead of being set per instance
`to_dict()` returns a `datetime` in the payload, which is not JSON-serializable for Celery kwargs and can raise a `TypeError` or be coerced unexpectedly
`from_dict()` can accept a serialized `queued` value as a string and construct an `AssignmentSource` where `queued` is a `str`, silently violating the intended `datetime` type contract

Calling `installation.should_sync("outbound_assignee", parsed_assignment_source)` with a new positional argument can break integration subclasses that still implement the old 1-parameter `should_sync(self, attribute)` signature, causing a `TypeError` at runtime

Uses Python 3.13-only APIs (`queue.ShutDown` exception and `Queue.shutdown(immediate=False)`) so on Python <=3.12 shutdown raises `AttributeError`, queues never close, and worker threads can block forever on `get()`
Shutdown joins worker threads with a timeout while workers are daemon threads, so timed-out joins can lead to threads being torn down mid-processing and in-flight work being dropped
Graceful shutdown closes/cancels workers without draining already-submitted queue items, leaving offsets in `OffsetTracker.outstanding` and preventing the commit loop from advancing
Because outstanding offsets aren’t drained/committed on shutdown, restarts re-read and reprocess queued offsets, causing duplicate uptime incident/issue evaluations
`wait_until_empty()` exists but is not called from `SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy.close()/terminate()` or `ResultsStrategyFactory.shutdown()`, so shutdown does not wait for queues to drain

`OffsetTracker` dictionaries (`all_offsets`, `outstanding`, `last_committed`, `partition_locks`) never evict entries on partition revocation, causing unbounded growth/leaked locks and state across rebalances

`FixedQueuePool.submit()` records an offset via `add_offset()` before the work item is safely enqueued; if `put()` or grouping/hash logic raises, offsets can become committable even though the work item was never processed (silent loss)
`_commit_loop` can exit on shutdown without performing a final `get_committable_offsets()`/commit pass, dropping the last chunk of commit progress due to a shutdown race
OptimizedCursorPaginator uses a negative cursor.offset as the slice start in queryset[start_offset:stop], triggering Django AssertionError for negative indexing and crashing paginated requests
BasePaginator.get_result allows a negative offset to reach queryset slicing when cursor.is_prev is true (start_offset not clamped), enabling a trivially reachable DoS across paginated endpoints
Lua add-buffer script uses ZUNIONSTORE without specifying AGGREGATE MAX (defaults to SUM), inflating scores for spans present in both sets and corrupting eviction ordering
Cursor.__init__ no longer validates non-negative offsets, allowing attacker-controlled cursor strings to inject large negative offsets into pagination logic

Unvalidated negative cursor offsets could cause incorrect behavior on non-QuerySet sequences (Python negative slicing semantics), potentially exposing unintended tail elements (cross-tenant/data window exposure risk)
Audit-log optimized pagination authorization gate is too permissive (allows has_global_access members), enabling non-staff users to trigger the advanced pagination path and associated risks

Removal of max_segment_spans guard in _load_segment_data eliminates defense-in-depth/telemetry against oversized segments, allowing unbounded segment loads if Lua eviction is bypassed or legacy data exists
Git submodule `sentry-repo` is added as a gitlink but `.gitmodules` is not updated, so `git submodule update --init`/CI checkouts will fail to resolve it

`_is_error_focused_query` uses a naive substring check (`"event.type:error" in query.lower()`), misclassifying negated/compound/quoted/unrelated queries as error-focused and incorrectly rewriting to `upsampled_count()`

60-second allowlist eligibility cache can keep upsampling enabled for up to 60 seconds after a project is removed from the allowlist, and `invalidate_upsampling_cache` is not wired to any option-change path

Cache key uses Python `hash(tuple(...))`, which is process-local/unstable for non-int tuple contents and can cause different workers to generate different keys (defeating caching) if `project_ids` ever include non-ints
`_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling` uses `except Exception: pass`, silently swallowing unexpected errors and potentially causing tests to skip setting `sample_rate` without noticing
An unrelated user-facing typo/message change is mixed into the feature PR, increasing diff noise and complicating reverts/review
`transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling` is redundantly called in multiple branches in `_get_event_stats`, risking future branch inconsistencies and unnecessary duplication

`upsampled_count` (`toInt64(sum(sample_weight))`) assumes `sample_weight` is non-null for all rows; NULL `sample_weight` rows will be skipped by `sum`, causing silent undercounting unless handled (e.g., `ifNull`/`sumIf`) or enforced end-to-end

Denial-cache key construction in userPermDenialCacheKey uses '_' as a delimiter even though components may contain '_', causing cache-key collisions between different (action,name,parent) tuples and incorrect denied results
Permission denial cache entries are never invalidated/purged when permissions are later granted, so previously denied users can remain blocked until shortCacheTTL expires
permissionCacheUsage("false") metric is emitted for cache-hit-but-denied cases as well as true cache misses, silently changing metric semantics and inflating the 'false' counter

Check treats any error from getCachedIdentityPermissions as a cache miss, swallowing real errors (e.g., GetUserIdentifiers failures/unsupported identity type), causing redundant failing lookups and misattributed metrics/logging
Uncaught HTTPError (e.g., 401 Unauthorized) from get_user_info() propagates and crashes the install flow with a 500 instead of rendering a graceful error page
Unguarded access to integration.metadata["sender"]["login"] can raise KeyError when metadata is missing or differently shaped, causing a 500 (and potential info disclosure)

Race condition: OAuth callback may execute before the installation webhook persists integration.metadata["sender"]["login"], causing false “Invalid installation request” failures or KeyError crashes for legitimate installs
Removal of FORWARD_INSTALL_FOR constant from pipeline_advancer.py can break external/internal importers via ImportError at import time
OAuth state uses pipeline.signature (not a fresh per-request nonce), enabling replay within the pipeline window and weakening CSRF protection
OAuth state comparison uses plain != instead of constant-time comparison (e.g., hmac.compare_digest), creating a potential timing side-channel
Test test_github_user_mismatch mutates the webhook payload but uses a hardcoded HMAC signature that won’t match the mutated body, so webhook validation will fail and the test will break
installation_id can be overwritten on the OAuth callback leg (query param preferred over bound state), allowing mid-flow installation swap and undermining identity-binding checks
Bare except Exception during OAuth token exchange parsing silently swallows real failures (network/parse/programming errors) without logging, hindering debugging and incident response
Test cleanup in OrganizationCacheTest registers literal "alias" instead of the loop alias variable, so created IDPs are never removed and tests can become flaky due to leaked realm state
The later creation of "idp-alias-20" also lacks correct cleanup registration, further leaking an IDP across test runs

InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.getForLogin() returns raw/unwrapped IdentityProviderModel instances on cache hits but returns OrganizationAwareIdentityProviderModel-wrapped instances on cache misses/fallback, causing inconsistent behavior depending on cache state

Login cache invalidation is not triggered when an IDP’s organization membership changes via OrganizationProvider addIdentityProvider/removeIdentityProvider (i.e., changes not going through IdentityProviderStorageProvider.update()), leaving stale cached login IDP sets
IdentityProviderStorageProvider.getLoginPredicate() dereferences idp.getConfig() without a null check, risking an NPE if getConfig() can be null on create/update/remove invalidation paths

getLoginPredicate() must remain aligned with JpaIdentityProviderStorageProvider.getForLogin() DB filtering; if they diverge, cache invalidation can miss relevant IDPs and allow stale entries to persist
Test scenario 4 in OrganizationCacheTest conflates update() and addIdentityProvider(), so it cannot verify that addIdentityProvider() alone triggers cache invalidation
Tests lack coverage that removing a login-eligible IDP invalidates the login caches (current removal test uses a disabled/non-eligible IDP, so it doesn’t assert the affirmative invalidation behavior)
`enableSqlExpressions` always returns false because both branches return `false`, making the feature-flag lookup computed-and-discarded

`enableSqlExpressions` assigns `enabled := !IsEnabledGlobally(FlagSqlExpressions)`, inverting the meaning of the variable name and risking future maintainers re-enabling/inverting behavior incorrectly
No comment or explicit documentation ties `enableSqlExpressions` being always-disabled to CVE-2024-9264, making the security intent implicit and easy to undo
Missing regression test to ensure SQL expressions remain disabled even if `FlagSqlExpressions` is set true, allowing accidental re-enablement to slip through CI

`pkg/expr/sql/parser.go` retains a vulnerable `fmt.Sprintf`-based SQL string interpolation pattern with naive quote escaping, which is unsafe for DuckDB and could reintroduce CVE-2024-9264 if execution is restored
Because the DuckDB wrapper is stubbed today, the vulnerable SQL builder remains as a dormant gadget that could become exploitable if `NewInMemoryDB` is later pointed back to a real engine
Error messages like `"sqlExpressions is not implemented"` / `"not implemented"` are misleading because the feature is intentionally disabled for CVE mitigation, not unimplemented
Lack of logging/metrics when rejecting SQL expressions means operators may see silent parse failures and have no signal for attempted use or migration impact

Double-checked locking is incomplete: after acquiring the write lock, the code does not re-check whether another goroutine has already populated entryPointAssetsCache, causing redundant asset loads during cold-start bursts

On error, the code unconditionally assigns entryPointAssetsCache = result even when result is nil, which can overwrite and clear a previously valid cache entry and force repeated reloads on subsequent calls

Cleanup ticker interval changed from 10 minutes to 1 minute in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go:77, likely an unintended debug leftover that increases cleanup frequency and can worsen DB lock contention/deadlocks

Six routine cleanup progress logs are emitted at Error level on the happy path in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:239, polluting error logs and triggering false alerts; they should be Debug/Info
One of the happy-path Error logs includes an "err" field where err is guaranteed nil, creating misleading log output
Happy-path logs include full ID slices ("ids", ids) for each batch, risking log flooding and increased ingestion costs on large annotation tables

SQLite >999-parameter branch builds the IN(...) list via repeated fmt.Sprintf string concatenation in a loop (O(n^2)) in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:323; should use strings.Builder or a more efficient approach
Cleanup interval is hardcoded (no config/feature flag/comment) in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go:77, removing an operator tuning knob; should be configurable and documented if changed intentionally

Async legacy write goroutines derive their context from the cancellable request context, so the context is canceled when the handler returns and legacy Create/Update/Delete/DeleteCollection writes are silently dropped
Create error path records a legacy-duration/error metric even though the failure occurred in storage (should record storage duration)
Update error path records a legacy-duration/error metric even though the failure occurred in storage (should record storage duration)
Delete passes the object name as the Prometheus 'kind' label, creating unbounded/high-cardinality metrics and risking OOM/scrape failures
DeleteCollection async legacy goroutine records storage duration instead of legacy duration, double-counting storage and leaving legacy histogram unpopulated
Update no longer wraps objInfo with an updateWrapper carrying the storage-persisted object, so Legacy recomputes the updated object from its own pre-image and can diverge from Storage
Delete attaches the base logger (d.Log) to the context instead of the enriched per-call logger, so downstream klog.FromContext(ctx) misses name/kind/method fields
Async goroutines lack panic recovery; a panic inside legacy driver/metrics code can crash the entire apiserver process
Tests reference a bare 'p' prometheus registry whose declaration may be missing after Mode 1 changes, potentially breaking compilation
Sharing a package-level/mutable Prometheus registry across tests (especially parallel subtests) can cause duplicate collector registration panics

Federated rule detection was removed in useAllRulerRuleAbilities (isFederated hardcoded to false), causing federated Mimir rule groups to be treated as mutable and enabling actions (Edit/Delete/Pause/etc.) that will fail against the backend

React key prop was dropped when replacing GrafanaRuleLoader with GrafanaRuleListItem in FilterView’s list rendering, leading to missing unique keys, runtime warnings, and unstable row identity/state across re-renders

Grafana rule detail links no longer include the returnTo query parameter in GrafanaRuleListItem, so navigating to a rule loses the filtered-list context and the Back flow no longer returns users to the same list state
TOCTOU race in CreateOrUpdateDevice: CountDevices check and subsequent insert/upsert are separate SQL statements without transaction/locking, allowing concurrent requests to exceed device_limit

Authenticate now calls TagDevice synchronously on the request context, so slow/stalled DB operations can block anonymous authentication indefinitely (or until DB timeout), increasing latency and enabling DoS amplification
Using the request context for TagDevice means client disconnect cancels ctx mid INSERT/UPDATE, potentially leaving inconsistent writes and generating cancellation errors
Removal of panic-recovery around TagDevice means a panic in the tagging path can crash the auth handler

updateDevice returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected==0, conflating device-not-found or stale-window update misses with actual device-limit enforcement, breaking error semantics/logging and auth-layer contract
Duplicate anonymousDeviceExpiration constant defined in both anonstore and api packages, creating two sources of truth that can drift and cause inconsistent behavior
When device_limit>0, CreateOrUpdateDevice performs an extra COUNT(*) round-trip on every anonymous request, adding high-RPS DB load and potential performance issues
Frontend anonymousDeviceLimit is typed as number|undefined while backend always sends a numeric field (0 when disabled), causing feature-gating logic based on undefined to behave incorrectly

I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! will raise NoMethodError at runtime if any environment file still sets config.i18n.fallbacks = true, because Rails after_initialize overwrites I18n.fallbacks with I18n::Locale::Fallbacks (which lacks ensure_loaded!)
Background jobs/rake tasks set I18n.locale without calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!, so fallback locales are never loaded outside web requests and translations may not fall back correctly
FallbackLocaleList does not implement I18n::Locale::Fallbacks#defaults, so I18n::Backend::Fallbacks may call I18n.fallbacks.defaults and raise NoMethodError during fallback resolution
FallbackLocaleList#[] calls SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym unconditionally, which will crash with NoMethodError when default_locale is nil (e.g., during boot/tests/settings unavailable)
traceID is no longer included in the "Plugin Request Completed" log line after switching to ContextualLoggerMiddleware, potentially breaking trace correlation
instrumentContext in ContextualLoggerMiddleware does not attach traceID to the context, so downstream logs may lose trace correlation unless infra logging adds it automatically
grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext silently falls back to d.New() when the underlying logger type assertion fails, wiping all contextual attributes without any warning/metric
TestLogger.FromContext returns a new disconnected NewTestLogger() instance, so logs emitted via FromContext won’t be captured by f.InfoLogs/f.ErrorLogs in tests
No tests were added for ContextualLoggerMiddleware to verify it instruments context (pluginId/endpoint/dsName/dsUID/uname) for each relevant endpoint wrapper, allowing regressions to slip through CI
Test function name remains TestInstrumentationMiddleware after renaming the file to metrics_middleware_test.go, creating stale/inconsistent test naming
SQL injection risk in migration db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb:18 due to interpolating admin-writable site_settings.value into raw INSERT statements
Migration can break or be exploited when a host value contains a single quote because the raw SQL INSERT is built via string interpolation
embeddable_hosts_controller uses EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first and then calls destroy/save on possibly-nil records, causing NoMethodError/500 instead of returning 404 on invalid IDs

embeddable_hosts_controller save_host reads params[:embeddable_host][:host] directly without strong parameters (require/permit) and without guarding when params[:embeddable_host] is missing, causing errors and unsafe mass assignment patterns
Authorization may be insufficient for embeddable host allowlist management because before_filter :ensure_staff may allow non-admin staff to modify the allowlist
EmbeddableHost model before_validation calls self.host.sub! unconditionally, causing NoMethodError when host is nil during validation

Frontend store hydration in app/assets/javascripts/discourse/models/store.js.es6:197 calls self.pluralize(...) but pluralize is not defined/imported, causing runtime TypeError and breaking _ids hydration
Frontend store hydration calls obj[k].map(...) without guarding against obj[k] being null/undefined, causing TypeError when server returns *_ids: null
Migration uses create_table :embeddable_hosts, force: true which can drop the table and silently delete all embeddable_host records if the migration is re-run

Fabricator definitions are in the wrong files: category_fabricator.rb contains :embeddable_host and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb contains category fabricators, creating a maintainability trap and potential duplicate-fabricator load errors
EmbeddableHost.record_for_host does not normalize hostname case (does not downcase URI(host).host) while querying with lower(host), causing allowlist mismatches for mixed/uppercase hosts
EmbeddableHost host validation regex caps TLD length at 5 characters, rejecting valid modern TLDs
EmbeddableHost host validation regex permits path suffixes (/(\/.*)?/), allowing stored host values with paths and increasing risk if reflected unsanitized
EmbeddableHost host validation regex does not reject '@' userinfo components in URLs

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the light-theme value for `.reply-count a` was incorrectly changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)`, unintentionally lightening the link color in light mode

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the dark-theme arm uses `scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)` but should be the inverted value `70%` (100-30) to match the intended dark-theme mapping

In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/modal.scss:102, the `dark-light-choose` arguments for `.custom-message-length` are inverted, causing light mode to use `$primary` at 30% instead of the original 70% (making it too dark in light theme)

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/user.scss:523, the light-theme value for `.name` was incorrectly changed from `$primary` lightness 30% to 50%, removing the intended visual distinction from the `.title` element in light mode
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/topic-post.scss:183, the light-theme value for `h3` was incorrectly changed from `$primary` lightness 20% to 50%, significantly lightening headings in light mode
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/user.scss:498, the light-theme value for `.name` was incorrectly changed from `$primary` lightness 30% to 50%, making it visually identical to `.title` in light mode
GroupsController#members uses an unbounded `limit` param, enabling clients to request extremely large pages and potentially cause a DoS via heavy serialization/memory use

Admin GroupsController `add_members`/`remove_member` mutate membership rows before `group.save`, so a later save failure can return an error while the DB membership changes have already been committed (non-transactional write)
Admin UI `totalPages` calculation is off-by-one when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit`, causing an extra empty page and incorrect pagination UI
`findMembers` returns `undefined` when group name is empty instead of a resolved promise/array, breaking callers that chain `.then(...)`
Group controller wire-format/param shape changes (create/update params and members response shape) are breaking and not versioned/deprecated, risking silent breakage for external consumers
Removed `/admin/groups/:id/users` route may still be referenced by plugins/themes or other code, causing 404s without a deprecation path or compatibility alias

Missing tests for new/changed behaviors: non-automatic group update with top-level params, add_members with nonexistent usernames, remove_member when user isn’t a member, and members pagination metadata across pages
Nitpick: `destroy` early-return uses `return };` (ASI-dependent stray semicolon/braces), inconsistent style; should be `return;`
Non-atomic read-modify-write of BlockedEmail.match_count in should_block? can lose increments under concurrent requests (race condition)
should_block? ignores the return value of record.save, potentially swallowing persistence failures
should_block? is called from EmailValidator#validate_each and can run multiple times per registration attempt (multiple valid? calls), causing match_count to be double-counted/inflated
Email validation builds a regex from SiteSetting by escaping only '.', allowing unescaped metacharacters that can enable ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking on signup
Email validation regex construction can raise RegexpError on misconfigured settings (e.g., '*.example.com'), causing signup to fail/DoS until fixed
create_account_controller pushes result.values.email without checking it exists, allowing undefined into rejectedEmails and causing spurious invalid state when accountEmail is undefined/empty
User profile template binds to model.website_name, but Discourse camelizes serializer fields to model.websiteName, causing website name to render empty/undefined

user_serializer.rb uses mutating concatenation ("." << website_host) on a string literal, which can raise FrozenError when frozen_string_literal is enabled and is semantically misleading for simple concatenation
user_serializer.rb parses URI(website.to_s) multiple times per call and only the first parse/host access is rescue-guarded; later .path calls are unguarded and can raise on malformed URIs

Docstring in user.js.es6 changed from @property websiteName to @property profileBackground, potentially removing/misleading documentation for an existing websiteName computed property and obscuring whether it exists on the User model

SSRF and potential command execution risk from using Ruby open-uri/Kernel#open on untrusted URLs (e.g., open(SiteSetting.feed_polling_url), open(url).read), allowing access to internal hosts and pipe-prefixed command execution
Anonymous users can influence @embed_url reaching TopicEmbed.import_remote via EmbedController#best → Jobs::RetrieveTopic → TopicRetriever#fetch_http, enabling untrusted URL fetching by visitors
Stored XSS risk because TopicEmbed.import stores posts with cook_method raw_html and Post#cook returns raw content verbatim, bypassing PrettyText/Sanitize, and the view renders raw post.cooked
embed.html.erb uses parent.postMessage with targetOrigin set to request.referer (a full URL), which is not a valid origin and can break the handshake
request.referer is interpolated into a single-quoted JavaScript string without escaping in embed.html.erb, enabling JS injection via a crafted Referer containing a quote
Parent-side postMessage origin validation in embed.js uses indexOf substring matching instead of strict origin equality, allowing spoofed origins to pass the check
Embed access control relies on Referer host comparison in ensure_embeddable, which is suppressible/spoofable and not a reliable authorization mechanism
Response sets X-Frame-Options: ALLOWALL, which is not a valid directive and won’t reliably control framing behavior
poll_feed.rb calls i.content.scrub on RSS items that may not have <content>, causing NoMethodError and aborting processing; should fall back to description and coerce to string
Redis throttling uses SETNX followed by a separate EXPIRE, which can leave a permanent key without TTL if a crash/failover occurs between commands
Migration uses create_table ... force: true, which can drop existing data when migrations are replayed/reset; committed migrations should not be destructive on rerun
Concurrent imports of the same embed_url can race: both workers may create TopicEmbed, the loser hits RecordNotUnique, causing a 500 and potentially leaving an orphan topic
app/views/embed/best.html.erb contains '<%- end if %>', which is a Ruby syntax error and prevents the template from rendering

Duplicate OptimizedImage.downsize method definitions cause the second definition to silently override the legacy 4-argument signature, breaking existing callers and potentially crashing when opts is an Integer
Server-side remote URL download limit is hardcoded to 10MB in uploads_controller.rb, bypassing admin-configured SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb and enabling larger-than-policy downloads
Client-side upload size checks and 413 messaging are hardcoded to 10MB in utilities.js, ignoring admin max_<type>_size_kb/max_image_size_kb settings and ignoring the upload type
OptimizedImage.downsize now accepts an unvalidated dimensions string that flows into shell-executed ImageMagick commands, widening shell-injection/RCE risk if user-controlled input ever reaches it
Downsize retry loop in uploads_controller.rb lacks convergence/failure checks (doesn’t break on no progress or failed convert), potentially exhausting attempts and still uploading an oversized file
Downsizing in-place by using the same tempfile.path for source and destination risks file corruption/0-byte output and stale size checks; should write to a separate temp file and replace atomically

Invoking ImageMagick convert up to 5 times per upload increases resource-exhaustion and ImageTragick attack surface; should validate image safety (MIME/pixels) and enforce ImageMagick policy limits/disable risky coders
Unsubscribe action uses TopicUser.find_by which can return nil, causing NoMethodError when accessing tu.notification_level and calling tu.save! for users without an existing TopicUser row

Unsubscribe endpoint is a GET route that mutates server state (tu.save!), making it vulnerable to CSRF and accidental triggering by link previewers/URL scanners; should use confirmation GET + CSRF-protected POST and/or signed token

Locale string requires %{unsubscribe_url} interpolation, but many mailers using add_unsubscribe_link: true do not pass unsubscribe_url, causing I18n::MissingInterpolationArgument and potentially dropping email deliveries

dropdown-button.js.es6 concatenates unescaped title into HTML (buffer.push with raw string), enabling stored XSS via user-controlled topic titles; other interpolated fields in renderString may also be unescaped

unsubscribe.hbs uses triple-brace {{{stopNotificiationsText}}} to render an I18n string that interpolates an unescaped topic title, enabling XSS; should avoid triple braces and move markup to template
Adding render :show inside shared perform_show_response forces all callers to render the show template, creating confusing coupling and breaking future actions that reuse the helper

Unsubscribe action does not check topic authorization (missing guardian.ensure_can_see!) and lacks a nil-guard for missing topics, enabling private-topic existence probing and undefined behavior for invalid topic_id

SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId is called with empty data {}, making updateMany a no-op so Prisma @updatedAt never bumps and the cache-last-updated indicator never refreshes (packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts:1022)
scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses BSD/macOS sed in-place syntax (sed -i '' -E) which fails on GNU sed/Linux, breaking the script in typical CI environments (scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh:67)

connectedCalendars handler hardcodes new CalendarCacheRepository(), bypassing the feature-flagged CalendarCacheRepositoryMock and potentially hitting DB / causing runtime errors when calendar-cache is disabled (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/calendars/connectedCalendars.handler.ts:27)

deleteCacheHandler authorization only checks credentialId + userId and does not account for team-shared or delegation credentials, enabling IDOR/broken access control where users can delete cache for shared/org credentials (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/calendars/deleteCache.handler.ts:12)

Using `cacheStatusMap.get(...) || null` can incorrectly coerce falsy values; should use nullish coalescing `?? null` for correctness/future-proofing (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/calendars/connectedCalendars.handler.ts:35)
Working-hours boundary check computes both start and end from slotStartTime, never using slotEndTime, so slots that extend past workingHour.endTime can be incorrectly marked available
Day-off override check compares two Dayjs instances with === (reference equality), so the branch never triggers and full-day unavailability overrides are ignored, leaving blocked days bookable
utcOffset is multiplied by -1, inverting the timezone shift direction and causing date-override matching to use the wrong local day/time
(Low confidence) DST boundary handling reuses the UTC offset sampled at override.start for override.end, which can be off by ±60 minutes across DST transitions
deleteMany cleanup predicate uses `retryCount > 1` without filtering `method: SMS`, so the SMS cron can delete EMAIL/WHATSAPP reminders from the shared `WorkflowReminder` table

If Twilio send succeeds but the subsequent Prisma update fails, the catch path increments `retryCount` without marking the reminder scheduled or saving the SID, causing the SMS to be resent (duplicate SMS) on the next cron tick

The `await prisma.workflowReminder.update(...)` inside the catch block is unguarded; if it throws, it propagates out and aborts the remaining cron loop iterations and may prevent sending the HTTP response

`retryCount` is incremented via non-atomic read-modify-write (`reminder.retryCount + 1`) based on a stale value from `findMany`, so concurrent cron runs can lose increments; should use Prisma atomic `increment`
Retry deletion threshold `retryCount > 1` is an unclear/off-by-one magic number (ambiguous intended number of attempts); should use clearer predicate (`gte`/different threshold) and a named constant
`retryCount` column is global on `WorkflowReminder` but only SMS handler uses it, creating method-specific semantics hidden in the schema and risking divergence/confusion with email/WhatsApp handlers
Exhausted reminders are silently deleted with no logging/metrics, reducing observability into failed deliveries
Logging raw Twilio error objects via `console.log` may leak sensitive data (e.g., phone numbers/message bodies) into logs

Public InsightsBookingService.findMany() method was removed and replaced with getBaseConditions(), potentially breaking external callers and forcing them to reimplement where-composition around raw SQL without a documented migration/caller-graph update
Constructor public options type allows scope:"team" without teamId, but the internal zod discriminated union requires teamId for team scope, causing runtime ZodError despite compiling

Caching test suite (describe("Caching" ...)) was deleted while caching fields/guards (cachedAuthConditions/cachedFilterConditions) remain in production code, reducing coverage and risking silent memoization regressions

Tests assert expect(conditions).toEqual(Prisma.sql`...`) on freshly-built Prisma.Sql fragments, which is fragile because it depends on Prisma.Sql internal structure that may change across Prisma versions and may not reliably pass
Webhook handler trusts body-supplied userId, allowing any caller with the webhook secret to overwrite any user's stored OAuth credentials (IDOR/broken access control)
Encryption key is passed as process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY || "", so an unset env var results in an empty-string crypto key and trivial/known-key decryption acceptance
Webhook secret check can be bypassed when CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset because undefined header compared to undefined secret skips the 403 branch, allowing unauthenticated requests
Webhook secret is compared with a non-constant-time string comparison, enabling timing attacks to recover the secret
No rate limiting on the credential-writing webhook, making timing attacks and request-flood DoS practical
No body-size cap on the webhook, allowing oversized payloads and increasing DoS risk
parseRefreshTokenResponse overwrites missing refresh_token with the literal string "refresh_token", poisoning stored credentials when providers omit refresh_token on refresh
refreshOAuthTokens sync branch returns a raw fetch Response instead of parsed JSON and does not check response.ok/content-type, causing parseRefreshTokenResponse to fail/crash or persist invalid data
Webhook endpoint lacks an HTTP method guard, so non-POST methods can reach parsing/decrypt/upsert logic and may leak secrets via logs/caches
symmetricDecrypt and JSON.parse are unguarded; malformed ciphertext/non-JSON causes 500s with distinguishable errors, creating an oracle and leaking details via status/timing differences
refreshOAuthTokens fetches CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT without protocol/host validation, creating SSRF/misconfiguration risk and enabling attacker-controlled token responses to be persisted
When credential sync is enabled but userId is null, refreshOAuthTokens silently falls back to direct refresh with no logging, hiding sync bypass/failures
minimumTokenResponseSchema only defines access_token but code mutates/relies on refresh_token, making the returned type inaccurate and potentially breaking callers/type-safety

`deleteScheduledEmailReminder` with `immediateDelete` cancels the SendGrid scheduled send but returns without deleting/updating the corresponding `WorkflowReminder` DB row, orphaning stale reminders on reschedule

In `scheduleEmailReminders.ts`, a single failing `client.request` inside a loop-level try/catch aborts the entire cancellation loop, skipping remaining reminders and skipping the trailing `Promise.all` of DB deletes/updates, causing starvation/retries

In `handleCancelBooking.ts`, reminder-cancellation promises are started inside synchronous `.forEach` without `await`, so the handler can resolve before DB writes complete and errors can become unhandled rejections

The same fire-and-forget (dropped Promise) reminder-cancellation pattern exists in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx` and multiple call sites in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx`, losing `Promise.all` synchronization

In `handleNewBooking.ts`, wrapping `forEach(async ...)` in `try/catch` cannot catch async rejections, making the error handling ineffective and allowing unhandled rejections (outer catch is effectively dead code)
Permission check incorrectly uses logical AND between isTeamAdmin and isTeamOwner, making the team admin/owner authorization branch unreachable
Guest email blacklist check is case-sensitive, allowing blacklisted addresses to bypass via case variation
Existing-attendee deduplication compares emails case-sensitively, allowing duplicate attendee rows when casing differs
Email fan-out passes raw guests list to sendAddGuestsEmails instead of filtered uniqueGuests, causing re-emailing of existing/filtered recipients and wrong email template selection
AddGuests input schema has no maximum guest count (and no rate limiting), enabling email-bombing/spam and resource exhaustion
Email send failures are caught and logged with console.log without the error details and then swallowed, returning success even when notifications fail
Organizer add-guests email subject uses calEvent.attendees[0].name, which may be empty because newly added guests have name set to "", producing blank names in the subject

Authorization allows any attendee to add unlimited guests without organizer consent, enabling unauthorized guest additions and leakage of event/video-call details (including video-call password) to added recipients
Backup codes are stored using reversible symmetric encryption instead of being hashed (should store only KDF hashes like bcrypt/argon2/scrypt)
Backup-code consumption is not atomic (TOCTOU): concurrent login requests can reuse the same backup code before it is invalidated
Backup-code verification uses non-constant-time comparison via Array.indexOf, leaking timing information about match position/prefix
React state for backupCodes is initialized with useState([]), inferred as never[], erasing type safety for string[] backup codes
resetState() does not clear sensitive React state (backupCodes, backupCodesUrl, dataUri, secret), leaving plaintext credentials in memory after modal close/cancel
Blob URL created for downloading backup codes is not revoked on close/unmount, causing memory leak and prolonged exposure of plaintext backup codes via the object URL
EventManager dereferences mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration without guarding when evt.destinationCalendar is [] or null, causing a runtime TypeError crash

GoogleCalendarService updateEvent uses a tautological fallback find predicate comparing cal.externalId to a falsy externalCalendarId, so selectedCalendar becomes undefined and calendar.events.update is called with calendarId undefined

GoogleCalendarService deleteEvent repeats the same tautological fallback find predicate, so calendarId becomes undefined and calendar.events.delete is called with calendarId undefined (non-primary events not deleted)
LarkCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection
Office365CalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection

BaseCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, breaking subclasses (e.g., CalDAV/Apple) that inherit it and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection

handleNewBooking initializes evt.destinationCalendar to null in some cases; the COLLECTIVE branch then does evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...), which becomes a no-op and silently drops team destination calendars when organizer has no personal destinationCalendar

EventManager updateAllCalendarEvents can call updateEvent with an undefined credential after a DB refetch that doesn’t populate credential when app.slug is missing/uninstalled, causing updates to silently no-op
handleCancelBooking credential DB refetch omits including app.slug and mapping to appName, so downstream code sees credential.appName undefined and breaks logs/emails/metrics keyed on appName
handleNewBooking loadUsers dynamic-user branch no longer selects organization.slug, causing downstream user.organization?.slug to be undefined for dynamic bookings

handleNewBooking loadUsers host branch can return undefined instead of an array when there are no hosts and eventType.users is undefined/null, leading to crashes in callers that assume an array (e.g., slice/push)

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in the tRPC reschedule path (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx:553) are not awaited, so calendar deletions can race/finish after the mutation returns and errors become unhandled promise rejections

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in vital reschedule (packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions run fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in wipemycalother reschedule (packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions run fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Recurring-event deletion in handleCancelBooking (packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:460) uses forEach(async ...) over credentials, so recurring deletions are not awaited by the cancel response/Promise.all and failures are orphaned

getCalendarCredentials in packages/core/CalendarManager.ts:28 assigns calendar = getCalendar(credential) without awaiting, so the returned calendar field is a Promise<Calendar|null> that can be mistakenly treated as Calendar|null (truthy checks always pass, method calls break)

Dynamic import() calls stored directly in the app-store barrel object (packages/app-store/index.ts:1) execute eagerly at module initialization, defeating intended lazy loading and adding await overhead to lookups

getVideoAdapters in packages/core/videoClient.ts:21 now awaits appStore imports sequentially in a for...of loop, reducing parallelism and increasing latency compared to the prior parallel mapping approach

fetch_error_details pairs error_ids with events.values() relying on dict ordering from nodestore.get_multi, which may reshuffle and associate wrong event data; existing test only covers single error and won’t catch it

Trace-item attribute keys query no longer sends environment filter (selection.environments), potentially merging keys across environments if backend previously narrowed results; backend behavior needs confirmation or documentation/update

Detector validator sets owner_user_id/owner_team_id directly from request-resolved ActorField without explicit same-organization validation, risking cross-org actor reference/IDOR if ActorField isn’t scoped

schedule_webhook_delivery uses a 30s processing_deadline_duration which may be too short for the scheduler pass under load compared to drain tasks, causing timeouts/pages; should be increased and measured

Default project option sentry:autofix_automation_tuning changed from 'low' to 'off' without migration/release note/flag, potentially disabling autofix for projects that never set it (user-facing regression)

feedback_summaries.make_input_prompt changed from bulleted list to '------' delimiter, altering LLM prompt shape and risking delimiter collisions if user feedback contains '------'; should escape/choose safer delimiter and update evals/golden outputs
PR description is 'Test 5', providing no intent/scope context for reviewers
PR mixes many unrelated subsystems with minimal replay-deletion changes, making review and rollback difficult; should be split into smaller PRs

Migrations 0917 and 0920 are modified to early-return and reference 0921_convert_org_saved_searches_to_views_rerevised.py which is not in the diff; if not already on main, migration chain will be broken
